title: Authentication, Passwords, Sessions, Cookies, and JOSE · Inference Brief: Password Verifier Policy
summary: The server stores weak password material, keeps session identifiers across privilege changes, decodes JWTs without validating them, accepts arbitrary algorithms, and treats possession as authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts password verifier policy increment in credential verification and principal-construction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply modern password hashing, salt, work policy, input bounds, constant-behavior comparison, breach and reset controls, rehash migration, and secret separation.
Persist state and an outbox fact atomically, publish with deduplication and ordering assumptions, and reconcile stuck or duplicate delivery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Password Verifier Policy · Theory Password Verifier Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3161 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate bounded token claims and session rotation before constructing a principal; keep rollback safe during a storage expansion; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Password Verifier Policy contract for the supplied data.
